summary: Plan-mode PR inventory classification for 10 listed PRs. No shared canonical was invented. Security-sensitive PRs are routed to central security handling; already closed PRs are kept closed; remaining open non-security PRs are kept as independent work because each has unique scope and current merge/close blockers.

actions.reason: Unique useful feature PR, but not merge-ready and not closable under this plan shard.
Unique PR with useful code, but broad surface, dirty merge state, and proof/check failures block merge or close-style action.
Security-sensitive PR must be handled by central OpenClaw security review, not ProjectClownfish backlog cleanup.
Distinct bugfix PR should stay open, but proof, conflict/staleness, and review-comment blockers prevent merge/fixed-by closeout.
Already closed; no mutation should be planned.
Direct tool invocation over gateway crosses a security-boundary review path, so it must route to central security handling.
Looks like a viable independent PR, but plan mode and missing merge preflight mean keep it open for normal maintainer review.
Already closed; no mutation should be planned.
Distinct small bugfix PR should stay open pending proof; not a duplicate or close candidate.
Unique cron feature PR with current proof/check blockers; keep open rather than close or route.
